title: Gilia ocellata
intro: Gilia ocellata, also known as Blue Eyed Mary, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the honeysuckle family (Polemoniaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it a potential remedy for treating various health issues such as wound healing, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Gilia ocellata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Blue Eyed Mary include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat skin conditions, coughs, and colds.
taxonomy: Gilia ocellata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. This family is a group of plants commonly known as the "gloxinia" or "polemonium" family. The genus Gilia is a group of annual or perennial plants native to North America. Gilia ocellata, also known as the spotted gilia, is one of the species within this genus. It is native to the western United States and Mexico.
